evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure high performance of reports and dashboards by optimizing queries and data models
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate — accuracy, efficiency, scalability, data security/compliance, documentation — with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Implement data security best practices, including row-level security, to ensure data privacy and compliance
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Corporate real estate services BI role with a compliance mention (data privacy) but no financial-reporting or regulatory-audit language, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Develop and optimize robust data models to ensure accuracy, efficiency, and scalability of reporting solutions
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The role optimizes and scales existing BI solutions within an established corporate services function, indicating a growing but not fully specialized data practice.
evidence.jd_authorship: Connect to, integrate, and transform data from multiple sources (e.g., SQL databases, Excel, BigQuery) using Power Query
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (Power BI Desktop, Power Query, Power Pivot, DAX, BigQuery) with precise application context make the day-to-day work clear, though some bullets are generic ('curiosity and a proactive approach').
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: collaborates with various business units to gather reporting requirements
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centers on delivering BI/reporting solutions to internal business consumers, placing it in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Work closely with the business to understand requirements and workshop solutions
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs are largely execution-oriented (create, maintain, connect, implement, assist) delivering scoped BI solutions rather than setting broader data strategy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No testing, data contracts, observability, or data quality language appears anywhere in the JD.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Implement data security best practices, including row-level security, to ensure data privacy and compliance
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: data privacy and compliance are mentioned but as one operational safeguard among many delivery-focused bullets, not a dominant risk narrative.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Develop and optimize robust data models to ensure accuracy, efficiency, and scalability of reporting solutions
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Dominant verbs are optimizing, maintaining, and improving existing BI solutions rather than building new infrastructure from scratch.
